articles.title: How tech workers are feeling in 2026: a workforce splitting in two
articles.summary: 55.7% of tech workers now report significant burnout, up from 44.7% a year ago, while career optimism has dropped to 48.7% from 54.8%. Only 22% cite losing their job to AI as a top fear, and the correlation between AI replacing parts of a job and layoff worry sits at essentially zero. What's actually driving the anxiety is the squeeze, since AI raised the bar for output and the reward has been more work for the same pay rather than fewer hours. More than half of respondents say they would steer a newcomer away from their own field, producing an average career recommendation score of -39.

articles.title: AI Overviews are expanding across commercial intent search
articles.summary: AI Overviews appeared on 71% more commercial-intent search results, expanding well beyond the informational queries where they first took hold. Finance saw the sharpest jump, up 231% in the period, followed by computers and electronics at 108% and games at 77%. Transactional queries moved in the opposite direction, with AI Overview appearance falling 5% as Google increasingly reserves them for the research phase of a purchase rather than the moment someone is ready to buy. Google Ads and AI Overviews are also showing up together roughly twice as often as a year ago, putting more paid and AI-generated real estate on the same results page.

articles.title: Turn daily moments into viral LinkedIn posts
articles.summary: Content works better when it starts with a relatable moment because personal stories stand out in a feed full of AI-generated advice. Start with a real event, build one clear message, and match the post to one of three goals: personal, proof, or promo. The STORM structure gives each post a simple flow from hook to takeaway and helps readers stay engaged. Specific hooks and a closing that connects back to the opening create stronger engagement and clearer calls to action.

articles.title: Brands see content gold in microdramas. Will they muck up the moment?
articles.summary: Microdramas give brands a low-cost way to build attention through entertainment instead of traditional ads, but success depends on strong storytelling before product promotion. Short episodes that open with a hook, end with a cliffhanger, and include clear business goals can create stronger engagement and commerce opportunities. Audience interest is growing fast as time spent with short drama apps increased by 5.78 billion hours in 2025, while Crocs generated nearly 10 million views from its first branded microdrama. Start with small tests, measure repeat viewing and binge behavior instead of only impressions, and avoid forcing products into the story because audiences quickly reject content that feels like an infomercial.

articles.title: See how content from social and video platforms performs on Google Search
articles.summary: Google Search Console now lets marketers measure how social and video content performs in Google Search and Discover across Instagram, TikTok, X, and YouTube. The new platform properties show which search queries generate clicks and impressions for individual posts, so teams can find the content and topics that attract search traffic. The Insights report gives a quick view of traffic trends and top posts, and the Performance report lets users filter, sort, and export the data for deeper analysis. This gives brands a single place to measure search visibility across owned websites and major social platforms.

articles.title: Paid Ads Playoff: Proven B2B Plays That Turn Spend Into Pipeline (Webinar)
articles.summary: This live session on July 10 brings B2B marketers together to break down paid ads that drive qualified pipeline. Speakers quickly share top-performing ad examples and results, then face rapid Q&A before the audience votes on which plays deserve a deeper dive. The format is interactive, with attendees able to ask questions and influence what gets explored further, ending with a final crowd-selected standout.

articles.title: How We're Keeping Reddit Real and Safe in the AI Era
articles.summary: Reddit is using AI-driven systems to proactively detect spam, bots, and harmful content before users see it. These upgrades now block 23M spam views daily, cut user exposure to spam by 20%, and revoke nearly 2M fake votes per day. Enforcement on hate and violent content has also dropped to under five seconds, reducing exposure by over 40%.

articles.title: It's all about framing
articles.summary: Framing can increase customer value as much as product quality because the right partner changes how people perceive and use an offer. Robinhood generated more than 50% of Kalshi's trading volume by presenting prediction markets inside an investing app, which made larger wagers feel normal to investment-minded users. Partnerships can also reposition a brand, as Apple used Hermès to present the Apple Watch as a luxury accessory and New Balance used fashion collaborations to change its image. Products are easier to copy than brand perception, so partnerships that expand reach and create stronger mental associations can create a lasting advantage.

articles.title: Netflix invented binge-watching. Now it may have outgrown it
articles.summary: Viewers are increasingly abandoning popular Netflix shows before a second season, driven by frequent cancellations, long gaps between seasons, and algorithm-shaped programming. Netflix's binge model was built to beat linear TV, a fight streaming already won when it overtook combined broadcast and cable viewing in 2025, but the platform now competes with short-form video and microdrama apps instead.

articles.title: The Rise of Ragebait Lit
articles.summary: Buzz-heavy memoirs and novels are sparking intense online debate. Group chats, Substack, and book clubs are driving the conversation. These books center on messy personal lives and polarizing choices. Readers engage for the drama but often feel personally invested in the authors, which sharpens reactions. Conversations move beyond hot takes into questions about feminism, relationships, and autonomy. The trend echoes the confessional era but feels more fractured today. Cultural regression shapes the tone, turning outrage into a way to process collective anger.

articles.title: OpenAI can generate ChatGPT Ads for you
articles.summary: ChatGPT Ads now offers to generate a complete ad, pulling from an advertiser's website and campaign settings, then handing it back for review, editing, and approval before it goes live.

articles.title: The 5 AEO Automations I'm Running This Month
articles.summary: These include weekly citation gap sweeps, auto-refreshing pages that convert, and the ship-it-twice LinkedIn system.

articles.title: 10 low-lift email tips
articles.summary: Use separate subdomains for transactional and marketing emails, clean your database regularly, and apply more precise suppression logic.
